categories.name: IF YOU'RE SAPPY & YOU KNOW IT

questions.clue: Here's a kernel of truth--this 5-letter adjective now used for something sappy was used by Chaucer to describe malty alcohol
This tearful word is an alteration of magdalene but has one less syllable
It's an alliterative type of article in which writers jerk tears from their readers with tales of sad human interest
You can use this word to describe tacky motel decor but also, literally, a calzone or fondue

final.clue: In order of fame, the first Cabinet was Jefferson (later prez), Hamilton ("my shot" guy), Knox (of fort fame), this attorney general
